title: Paul's First Missionary Journey
description: A compact route from Antioch in Syria through Cyprus and southern Galatia, suitable for validating the 3D Journey data model beyond patriarchal routes.
caution: This route compresses several travel legs and should be treated as a teaching outline rather than a precise cartographic reconstruction.

stops.name: Antioch in Syria
stops.description: The sending church from which Barnabas and Saul were commissioned.
stops.notes: Primary base of operations at the start of the mission.

stops.name: Seleucia
stops.description: The port from which the missionaries sailed to Cyprus.
stops.notes: A practical port marker used to model the embarkation leg.

stops.name: Salamis
stops.description: The first major Cypriot stop in the journey.
stops.notes: Beginning of the island preaching circuit.

stops.name: Paphos
stops.description: The western city where the proconsul encountered the gospel message.
stops.notes: A key narrative point because of the encounter with the proconsul.

stops.name: Perga
stops.description: The entry point into Asia Minor after leaving Cyprus.
stops.notes: Commonly treated as a transition point from island travel to inland movement.

stops.name: Pisidian Antioch
stops.description: A major inland preaching location where Paul addressed the synagogue.
stops.notes: Often highlighted for Paul's synagogue sermon in Acts 13.

stops.name: Iconium
stops.description: A city where the message spread and opposition intensified.
stops.notes: Represents continued inland ministry and opposition.

stops.name: Lystra
stops.description: The setting of the healing of the lame man and later persecution.
stops.notes: A vivid miracle-and-opposition scene in Acts 14.

stops.name: Derbe
stops.description: A final inland stop before retracing the route home.
stops.notes: Commonly treated as the eastern extent of the first journey.

stops.name: Attalia
stops.description: The coastal port used on the return toward Syrian Antioch.
stops.notes: Acts mentions the port as part of the return journey.

segments.label: Commissioned and sent
segments.description: The missionaries leave Antioch and head for the coastal port.
segments.caution: The local overland details are simplified for a teaching map.

segments.label: To Cyprus
segments.description: The mission sails to Cyprus and arrives at Salamis.
segments.caution: Maritime travel is reduced to a single segment for clarity.

segments.label: Across Cyprus
segments.description: Travel across the island to the Roman administrative center at Paphos.
segments.caution: The island route is simplified to one direct line.

segments.label: Sailing to Pamphylia
segments.description: The party sails from Cyprus to the Anatolian coast.
segments.caution: The return voyage is compressed to a single port-to-port leg.

segments.label: Into Galatia
segments.description: The route moves inland toward Pisidian Antioch.
segments.caution: The inland road network is approximated by one representative leg.

segments.label: Continued preaching
segments.description: The missionary work continues eastward to Iconium.
segments.caution: Exact route choice is not specified in Acts.

segments.label: Pressing onward
segments.description: The journey proceeds to Lystra after opposition increases.
segments.caution: The route is summarized to support the map MVP.

segments.label: To Derbe
segments.description: The missionaries travel onward to Derbe.
segments.caution: The road between Lycaonian cities is generalized.

segments.label: Homeward leg
segments.description: The return route heads toward the coast.
segments.caution: The reverse journey is simplified to a single leg.

segments.label: Return to Antioch
segments.description: The mission concludes back at the sending church in Antioch.
segments.caution: Acts compresses the final return into a brief narrative summary.
